message:
test(replay): include sidecar fixtures in corpus assertion

Close beads_rust-rsei.2.

files.patch:
@@ -542,7 +542,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","title":"Inventory and classify real-world workspace failure modes","description":"## Purpose
Create the canonical catalog of field-relevant failure modes instead of treating each new corruption report as a novel surprise.

## Include
- `beads.db` corruption classes: not-a-database, malformed schema, short reads, index mismatch, sidecar mismatch, duplicate schema entries, partially recoverable row-level corruption.
- JSONL issues: conflict markers, malformed lines, stale export, missing updates, mixed prefixes.
- Metadata/path drift: wrong db/jsonl target, missing or stale metadata, routed path confusion, ambient env overrides.
- Lifecycle interruption issues: crash during create/update/delete/sync/rebuild, partial temp-file cleanup, interrupted sidecar updates.
- Multi-actor issues: concurrent writers, interleaved reads+writes, multiple agents on one workspace, existing HOME/BEADS env state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every class has: symptom signature, likely root cause, observability surface, data-loss risk, and desired system response.
- The taxonomy is concrete enough to drive fixtures and tests in later beads.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:22.876629145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:17:37.551883972Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:17:37.540578522Z","close_reason":"Documented the canonical workspace failure-mode taxonomy in docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md with concrete symptom/root-cause/observability/risk/response mappings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","incidents","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:22.876629145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":189,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self note: do not let this devolve into a vague document. The point is to enumerate the exact workspace states we expect to survive, reject, quarantine, or rebuild from, using the failures users actually hit plus the states implied by the current code and troubleshooting docs.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:23Z"},{"id":215,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CreamBasin bootstrap complete. Finished full AGENTS/README pass, mapped the main architecture, claimed this bead, and started compiling a concrete workspace-failure taxonomy from the existing docs/code paths before editing the docs surface.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:15:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","title":"Encode an executable workspace health contract and invariant matrix","description":"## Purpose
Turn the taxonomy into an executable health contract so code and tests can assert specific invariants instead of guessing.

## Cover
- Required agreements among SQLite, JSONL, metadata, and sidecars.
- What counts as recoverable vs unrecoverable.
- Which derived tables may be rebuilt and which source-of-truth tables may not be silently discarded.
- What `doctor`, startup/open, write recovery, and sync status must each report for the same workspace.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Invariants are specific enough to encode in tests and diagnostics.
- The contract explicitly names acceptable automatic recovery actions and forbidden silent behaviors.
- Derived-state rebuild rules are separated from primary-data repair rules.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:24.216654762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:23:04.925232727Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:23:04.912510332Z","close_reason":"Documented the executable workspace health contract and invariant matrix in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md so later diagnostics/tests can assert the same repair and reporting rules.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","invariants","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:24.216654762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:46.665559578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":190,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reasoning: the project already has implicit contracts spread across open/recovery/sync/doctor paths. This bead makes them explicit so later work can fail fast when one surface says 'healthy' and another surface proves otherwise.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:25Z"},{"id":216,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CreamBasin continuing on the next unblocked bead. I validated that the failure-taxonomy content landed on the troubleshooting surface, so I am taking this follow-on task to encode the explicit workspace health contract and invariant matrix in the architecture docs.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:19:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","title":"Define the incident bundle capture workflow for field failures","description":"## Purpose
Define the minimal reproducibility bundle we should gather the first time a field failure is reported so we stop losing crucial evidence.

## Bundle Contents
- failing command + stdout/stderr
- `br doctor --json`
- `metadata.json`
- `issues.jsonl`
- presence and hashes of `beads.db`, `beads.db-wal`, `beads.db-shm`, `beads.db-journal`
- environment overrides that affect discovery/path/output
- whether multiple agents/processes were active

## Acceptance Criteria
- The bundle is small enough to request quickly during incident response.
- The captured data is sufficient to classify the failure using the taxonomy from this track.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:25.615887382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:23:54.847666702Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:23:54.834341839Z","close_reason":"Defined the minimal incident evidence bundle for field failures in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["incident-response","ops","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:25.615887382Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:55.594416915Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists because ad hoc bug reports waste time and collapse into speculation. If we cannot preserve the evidence bundle, we cannot expect to reproduce or eliminate field failures systematically.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:26Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","title":"Build a sanitized real-world workspace failure corpus and replay harness","description":"## Why
Hermetic tempdir tests are not enough. We need a standing corpus of realistic damaged or drifted workspaces and a way to replay them automatically.

## Scope
Create sanitized fixtures that model the important bad states and wire them into a replay harness that exercises open/read/write/doctor/sync behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The corpus includes the representative classes from the taxonomy.
- The replay harness can be run locally and in CI.
- Failures in the corpus are easy to inspect and update intentionally.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:27.047082628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:41.863684637Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixtures","reliability","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:27.047082628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:41.863085696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":192,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track converts anecdotal production pain into durable regression assets. The goal is not synthetic benchmark data; it is realistic workspace states that future code must handle safely.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:28Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","title":"Build a sanitized real-world workspace failure corpus and replay harness","description":"## Why
Hermetic tempdir tests are not enough. We need a standing corpus of realistic damaged or drifted workspaces and a way to replay them automatically.

## Scope
Create sanitized fixtures that model the important bad states and wire them into a replay harness that exercises open/read/write/doctor/sync behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The corpus includes the representative classes from the taxonomy.
- The replay harness can be run locally and in CI.
- Failures in the corpus are easy to inspect and update intentionally.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:27.047082628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:54:52.959101811Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:54:52.958762626Z","close_reason":"Workspace failure fixture corpus and replay harness are in place; all child tasks are closed and workspace_failure_replay passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixtures","reliability","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:27.047082628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:41.863085696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":192,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track converts anecdotal production pain into durable regression assets. The goal is not synthetic benchmark data; it is realistic workspace states that future code must handle safely.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","title":"Create a sanitized fixture corpus for representative corrupt and drifted workspaces","description":"## Purpose
Build a curated, sanitized fixture corpus for representative workspace failure states.

## Required Fixture Families
- corrupt or partially corrupt `beads.db`
- `beads.db` + stale/missing `-wal`/`-shm` combinations
- malformed schema / duplicate schema-entry states
- JSONL with malformed lines or conflict markers
- DB/JSONL/metadata disagreement
- path/env discovery edge cases that only appear outside hermetic tests
- interrupted rebuild/sync/export leftovers

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fixtures are sanitized and safe to store in-repo.
- Each fixture includes metadata explaining the intended state and expected classification.
- The corpus is organized so future incidents can add new fixtures instead of spawning one-off repro scripts.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:29.035540341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T12:08:20.419604596Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T12:08:20.414239862Z","close_reason":"Added sanitized workspace-failure fixture corpus and loader support","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corruption","fixtures","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:04.515270731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:29.035540341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":193,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Key consideration: a fixture corpus only helps if it preserves the interesting structure of the failure. Do not over-normalize fixtures into clean states that no longer reproduce the real anomaly.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30Z"},{"id":220,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claiming this now. Initial focus: inventory existing malformed/corrupt workspace fixtures and repro directories already in-repo, identify the canonical fixture layout under tests/fixtures or adjacent harness code, then add a sanitized metadata-backed corpus that downstream reliability beads can consume.","created_at":"2026-03-12T11:33:27Z"},{"id":222,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed fixture corpus setup under tests/fixtures/workspace_failures/. Added seven small, metadata-backed workspace cases covering corrupt DB text, JSONL conflict markers, DB/JSONL drift, duplicate config rows, metadata-driven custom paths, WAL-without-SHM sidecars, and interrupted rebuild leftovers. Added loader/support APIs in tests/common/dataset_registry.rs so tests can enumerate fixtures and materialize a transport-safe visible beads/ payload back into runtime .beads/ workspaces. Verification: cargo fmt --check; rch exec -- cargo test workspace_failure_fixture -- --nocapture; rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets; rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","created_at":"2026-03-12T12:07:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","title":"Add a replay harness that asserts command behavior against the fixture corpus","description":"## Purpose
Add a harness that mounts each fixture workspace and asserts the expected behavior across the main operational surfaces.

## Required Surfaces
- startup/open
- read commands (`list`, `show`, `ready`, `where`, `info`)
- write commands (`create`, `update`, `close`, `reopen`, `label`, `comments`, `delete` as appropriate)
- `doctor`
- `sync --status`, import/export/rebuild behavior where applicable

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each fixture records expected classification and expected command outcomes.
- The harness distinguishes: healthy, degraded-but-usable, recoverable, and unsafe.
- Regressions are reported in a way that makes the failing fixture/state obvious.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30.673427289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:25:40.921032771Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:25:40.920735354Z","close_reason":"Replay harness verified: 4 tests pass (manifest expectations, core read, core write, classification coherence), 7 fixtures across 4 classification levels (usable, degraded, recoverable, unsafe), all required surfaces covered.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["reliability","replay","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:22.349991073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:30.673427289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:13.418006391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":194,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This is the bridge between plan-space and code-space. Once this bead lands, future corruption fixes should start by adding or updating a fixture expectation instead of inventing bespoke local repro steps.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:31Z"},{"id":226,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented explicit fixture command-outcome metadata plus a new tests/workspace_failure_replay.rs harness that replays fresh workspace-failure copies across startup/read/write/doctor/sync surfaces. Verified the manifest contract against the current target/debug/br on all seven fixtures. Cargo verification via rch is currently blocked by a stale worker-side /data/projects/frankensqlite checkout (fsqlite-core remote_effects.rs still calling bulkhead.cancel_entry(entry_id) without the new Time arg), so leaving this bead in_progress until offloaded cargo passes cleanly.","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:52:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","title":"Generate deterministic long-lived workspace evolution scenarios","description":"## Purpose
Create long-lived workspace scenarios that accumulate real history over time rather than relying only on one-shot fresh temp workspaces.

## Cover
- many commands over one workspace
- restarts between commands
- mixed auto-import/auto-flush behavior
- repeated derived-state rebuilds
- state after partial failures and subsequent recovery attempts

## Acceptance Criteria
- Scenario generator can produce deterministic seeds for reproducible failures.
- Generated workspaces can be fed back into the fixture/replay system.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:32.337089325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T16:00:01.697780433Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T15:59:50.012415288Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic long-lived workspace evolution generator and replay handoff","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["long-lived","reliability","state-drift"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:31.233437625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:32.337089325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:54:40.459575482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":195,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rationale: production users do not reinitialize  for every command. Long-lived state accumulation is where 'suite is green but field is broken' bugs are born.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:33Z"},{"id":224,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting now. Plan: add a deterministic long-lived workspace evolution generator in tests/common that can replay many-command histories across restarts, mixed auto-import/auto-flush modes, derived-state rebuilds, and recovery-after-failure paths. I’ll keep the output reusable so generated states can feed back into the fixture/replay corpus.","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:29:36Z"},{"id":227,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented a seeded long-lived workspace evolution layer in tests/common/scenarios.rs. It now supports deterministic multi-command workspace scripts with issue-ID capture, stale/no-auto-import flows, explicit flush/import cycles, JSONL corruption + snapshot restore, and materialization back into DatasetOverride/replay-style inputs. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed; isolated-target cargo check --all-targets passed; workspace_evolution test filter passed locally across the integration binaries that include tests/common. Repo-wide isolated-target clippy now fails only in tests/workspace_failure_replay.rs (parallel bead surface), not in this implementation.","created_at":"2026-03-12T16:00:01Z"}]}
@@ -553,7 +553,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","title":"Add a long-running single-workspace stress suite","description":"## Purpose
Create a long-running stress suite that reuses one workspace across many commands and processes to surface drift, stale assumptions, and accumulated-state bugs.

## Cover
- repeated issue lifecycle operations
- repeated sync/import/export status checks
- intermittent failures and restarts
- mixed human-style and agent-style command ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- The suite can run for a meaningful duration without manual babysitting.
- Failures leave behind enough artifacts to classify and reproduce.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41.731866221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:35:25.309497352Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:35:25.309127179Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic long-lived single-workspace stress suite with mixed create/update/label/comment/close/reopen/sync/read/expected-failure flow; verified targeted stress test, workspace evolution tests, fmt, check, clippy, and ubs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["long-lived","reliability","stress"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:28.600633433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:41.731866221Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:37.659700575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":200,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists because the product is a stateful CLI, not a stateless request handler. Bugs that require dozens or hundreds of operations on the same workspace will never show up in per-test fresh-init patterns.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","title":"Harden detection, recovery, and no-data-loss guarantees","description":"## Why
Even with better tests, the product must behave safely when it encounters a damaged or inconsistent workspace in production. Detection, recovery, and operator messaging need to be consistent and trustworthy.

## Scope
Unify anomaly classification, preserve evidence before repair, verify post-recovery state, and make `doctor`/startup/write behavior agree.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Automatic recovery is explicit, reversible, and validated.
- We do not report "OK" for workspaces that are only partially readable or that fail on writes.
- Recovery logic never silently hides potential data loss.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43.783459850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:09.162394901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","recovery","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:43.783459850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:59.944386891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:09.161785269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":201,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track is where field pain becomes product behavior. The product must either operate safely, recover safely, or stop with actionable evidence; the current gray zone in between is unacceptable.","created_at":"2026-03-12T02:52:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","title":"Unify anomalous workspace classification across startup, doctor, and write paths","description":"## Purpose
Create one canonical anomaly classifier that every entry point uses when evaluating a workspace: startup/open, explicit `doctor`, mutation paths, sync, and recovery logic.

## Scope
- define shared anomaly classes for malformed SQLite pages, WAL/SHM mismatch, JSONL parse drift, metadata contradictions, stale dirty/export state, and broken derived caches
- remove ad hoc command-specific interpretations of the same evidence
- ensure structured errors and operator-facing diagnostics use the same language

## Acceptance Criteria
- A given workspace snapshot receives the same classification regardless of which command hits it first.
- Recovery and write decisions are driven by named health states rather than scattered heuristics.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:49:21.619173305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:51:17.532667668Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:51:17.532378717Z","close_reason":"Created src/health.rs with canonical WorkspaceHealth/AnomalyClass/WorkspaceClassification types. Integrated into OpenStorageResult::classify() and doctor JSON output. 7 unit tests + replay harness tests pass. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["classification","diagnostics","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:47.622950600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:55:57.607408234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:49:21.619173305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead is the hinge point for the whole hardening track. If startup says “healthy enough” while writes or sync say “catastrophic corruption,” the user experiences chaos even when each individual code path looks locally reasonable.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:21Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","title":"Quarantine corrupt DB artifacts before automatic rebuild or repair","description":"## Purpose
When corruption or unsafe drift is detected, preserve the original DB artifacts before any rebuild, retry, or repair action mutates evidence.

## Scope
- move or snapshot suspect SQLite artifacts and sidecars into a clearly labeled quarantine area
- record why quarantine happened and what command triggered it
- make rebuild-from-JSONL and similar repair flows operate on fresh targets rather than on top of ambiguous debris

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery never silently overwrites the only copy of a damaged DB or WAL.
- Operators can inspect and attach quarantined artifacts to incident reports.
- Automatic repair paths become auditable and reversible.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:50:44.050503419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:24.853710150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["quarantine","recovery","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:50:44.050503419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:06.949746141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The root reliability principle here is evidence preservation. A “successful” automatic rebuild that destroys the original failing artifacts is still a product failure because it erases the operator’s ability to diagnose, prove, or safely roll back what happened.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:24Z"}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","title":"Quarantine corrupt DB artifacts before automatic rebuild or repair","description":"## Purpose
When corruption or unsafe drift is detected, preserve the original DB artifacts before any rebuild, retry, or repair action mutates evidence.

## Scope
- move or snapshot suspect SQLite artifacts and sidecars into a clearly labeled quarantine area
- record why quarantine happened and what command triggered it
- make rebuild-from-JSONL and similar repair flows operate on fresh targets rather than on top of ambiguous debris

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery never silently overwrites the only copy of a damaged DB or WAL.
- Operators can inspect and attach quarantined artifacts to incident reports.
- Automatic repair paths become auditable and reversible.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:50:44.050503419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:18.039367410Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["quarantine","recovery","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:50:44.050503419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:06.949746141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The root reliability principle here is evidence preservation. A “successful” automatic rebuild that destroys the original failing artifacts is still a product failure because it erases the operator’s ability to diagnose, prove, or safely roll back what happened.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","title":"Verify post-recovery invariants and forbid silent state loss","description":"## Purpose
Every recovery path must end with explicit postconditions proving that user-visible state and internal invariants are acceptable before normal writes continue.

## Scope
- verify issue graph, comments, labels, events, metadata, export markers, sidecars, and derived caches after repair
- detect silent data loss, ID collisions, truncated exports, stale `last_touched`, or self-contradictory counters
- refuse to declare success if postconditions fail

## Acceptance Criteria
- Recovery paths have a machine-checkable “safe to proceed” verdict.
- Silent partial repair is treated as failure, not success.
- Tactical corruption fixes roll into a shared invariant-validation framework.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:08.702874952Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:28.029445713Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["invariants","recovery","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:08.702874952Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:16.123909606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:25.768255051Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-v13g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:36.942963087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":205,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"A retry-after-rebuild path is only defensible if the product can prove the rebuilt workspace is coherent. This bead turns “it seems to work now” into explicit post-recovery evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","title":"Expand doctor to detect drift, sidecar anomalies, and partial-functionality traps","description":"## Purpose
Make `br doctor` the authoritative workspace inspection tool for drift and partial-breakage states, not just an optimistic health check.

## Scope
- detect DB/JSONL divergence, invalid or contradictory metadata, suspicious sidecar leftovers, and partially functioning workspaces where reads pass but writes fail
- expose actionable structured diagnostics and recommended next steps
- ensure doctor classification aligns with startup and mutation decisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- `doctor` can flag the classes of failures that users are actually hitting in the field.
- A workspace that is unsafe for writes is never reported as simply “OK.”","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:51.118030931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T13:55:06.498852500Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","doctor","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:35.332546461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:51.118030931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:44.594245547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The immediate user complaint is exactly this gap: the system can look healthy from one angle and then fail catastrophically on the next write. `doctor` has to become the honest reporter of those partial-functionality traps.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:31Z"},{"id":232,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress note: patched `br doctor` to add explicit `db.sidecars` error diagnostics and `db.recovery_artifacts` warnings, plus local sidecar quarantine-in-recovery behavior for `doctor --repair`. Added targeted unit tests and a new E2E case for anomalous WAL-without-SHM repair. Claim is still graph-blocked by beads_rust-rsei.4.1 / beads_rust-rsei.4, so this is progress without status transition until verification finishes.","created_at":"2026-03-12T18:29:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","title":"Fix spurious primary-key errors after successful JSONL-only writes","description":"## Problem
During this planning session, `br create --no-db`, `br comments add --no-db`, and `br dep add --no-db` successfully persisted changes to JSONL and then still returned `DATABASE_ERROR` with `PRIMARY KEY constraint failed`. That makes automation believe the write failed even though state changed underneath it.

## Repro Shape
- create or update beads in `--no-db` mode against the current long-lived workspace
- observe the issue/comment/dependency present afterward
- observe the command exit non-zero with a bogus primary-key error after the successful write

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSONL-only write commands return success when the write succeeded
- duplicate-write protection still works when a write genuinely conflicts
- tests cover issue creation, comment creation, and dependency creation in `--no-db` mode","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:00:57.722543395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T06:51:38.339885652Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T06:51:38.326075963Z","close_reason":"Reduced export_hashes rewrite batching to avoid false PRIMARY KEY failures after successful --no-db JSONL writes, and added storage/e2e regressions that cover create, comments add, and dep add.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["jsonl-only","reliability","write-path"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:00:57.722543395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":214,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead was discovered while creating the reliability program itself, which is exactly why it belongs here: the planning/tooling path should not be a special case that silently normalizes bad write semantics.","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:01:01Z"},{"id":217,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Root caused the false --no-db PRIMARY KEY failure to the export_hashes rewrite path, reduced the rewrite batch size, and added storage + e2e regressions for create/comments/dep add.","created_at":"2026-03-12T06:51:27Z"}]}
